suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: the_flash_fact
formats.instruction: Drop a single little-known fact, then immediately challenge the reader to guess why it's true. No follow-up explanation.

formats.name: mini_monologue
formats.instruction: Write a brief, impassioned rant or celebration on a topic, as if leaving a voicemail. Stream-of-consciousness, 3-6 sentences, no editing for coherence.

formats.name: sensory_diary
formats.instruction: Describe an experience only using sensory details (sight, sound, touch, taste, smell) — no abstract thoughts, just what you perceived. Minimum 5 distinct sensory moments.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Write about a commonly accepted tip, but explain why it DIDN'T work for you. Two paragraphs: your expectation, then your reality.

formats.name: time_machine_letter
formats.instruction: Compose a letter to your past self (at a specific age), focusing on one piece of wisdom or regret. Be vivid, intimate, and direct.

formats.name: image_catalogue
formats.instruction: List 6-8 short, poetic captions or 'alt texts' as if describing unseen images. Each line stands alone, but all relate to a single theme.

formats.name: metaphor_map
formats.instruction: Pick a topic, then invent three metaphors to explain it in radically different ways. For each, briefly connect the metaphor to reality.

formats.name: nested_narrative
formats.instruction: Tell a story within a story: Start with a scene, shift to a memory or dream that relates to it, then return to the original scene and reflect.

formats.name: trend_dissection
formats.instruction: Break down a recent cultural, tech, or social trend into three components: what started it, how it's evolving, and your prediction for its future.

title_styles: Write an evocative, visual title: use imagery or metaphor to hint at your topic.
Write a question-based title, designed to intrigue or challenge conventional wisdom.
Write a playful, paradoxical title that sounds impossible or contradictory.
Write a title with an emotional tone: surprise, awe, frustration, nostalgia—make a feeling the focus.
Write a cryptic title that gives only a clue, not the full topic.
structure_variants: Begin with a vivid sensory moment, then unpack its meaning.
Construct the post as a series of miniature flashbacks, each building toward a conclusion.
Organize ideas as alternating pros and cons, but end with a third, unexpected perspective.
Interleave personal narrative and commentary—switch between experience and analysis after each paragraph.
End with an open question, rather than a summary or conclusion.
topic_angles: Strange rituals and habits in daily life.
Moments when technology surprised you (for better or worse).
Experiences where creative thinking solved a real-world problem.
Instant judgments that turned out to be wrong.
